title: Dialogue Practice Bank
subtitle: B2-level conversations between a woman and a man on everyday topics

script.speaker: A
script.text: Did you see the new urban plan for the city centre?

script.speaker: B
script.text: I did. They want to reduce car traffic and add more bike lanes.

script.speaker: A
script.text: That sounds good, but shop owners are worried customers won't come.

script.speaker: B
script.text: That's what they said in other cities, but foot traffic actually increased.

questions.stem: What concern do shop owners have about the urban plan?
options: Bike lanes are too expensive to build.
Fewer customers will visit their shops.
The construction will take too long.
They prefer more parking spaces.
questions.explanation: The woman says shop owners are worried customers won't come if car traffic is reduced.

script.speaker: A
script.text: Are you going to the careers workshop this afternoon?

script.speaker: B
script.text: I was planning to, but they moved it to next Thursday.

script.speaker: A
script.text: Oh really? I was about to leave for the hall.

script.speaker: B
script.text: Yeah, the guest speaker missed her flight, so they rescheduled.

questions.stem: Why was the careers workshop rescheduled?
options: The hall was already booked.
The guest speaker missed her flight.
Not enough students signed up.
The organiser was sick.
questions.explanation: The man says the guest speaker missed her flight, so they moved it to next Thursday.

script.speaker: A
script.text: Did you manage to print the handouts for the meeting?

script.speaker: B
script.text: I tried, but the printer is completely out of paper.

script.speaker: A
script.text: That's frustrating. Did you check the supply cupboard?

script.speaker: B
script.text: Empty too. I ended up emailing everyone a digital copy instead.

questions.stem: How did the man solve the printing problem?
options: He borrowed paper from another office.
He bought paper from the shop downstairs.
He emailed everyone a digital copy.
He postponed the meeting.
questions.explanation: The man says he emailed everyone a digital copy instead of printing handouts.

script.speaker: A
script.text: I can't believe your phone still works after all these years.

script.speaker: B
script.text: Barely. The battery dies by midday, and the screen is cracked.

script.speaker: A
script.text: Why don't you just get a new one?

script.speaker: B
script.text: I'm waiting for next month when the newest model goes on sale.

questions.stem: What will the woman probably do next month?
options: Repair her current phone.
Buy the newest phone model.
Switch to a different phone brand.
Wait for the price to drop further.
questions.explanation: The woman is waiting for next month when the newest model goes on sale.

script.speaker: A
script.text: Could you close the window? The traffic noise is really bothering me.

script.speaker: B
script.text: I'd rather leave it open — it's quite warm in here.

script.speaker: A
script.text: I understand, but I can barely concentrate on my work.

script.speaker: B
script.text: Alright, I'll close it. We can open it again later when it cools down.

questions.stem: What does the man initially want to do?
options: Keep the window open.
Move to a quieter room.
Close the window partway.
Finish work outside.
questions.explanation: The man says he'd rather leave it open because it's warm.

script.speaker: A
script.text: How did your meeting with the academic advisor go?

script.speaker: B
script.text: Really well. She helped me choose courses for next semester.

script.speaker: A
script.text: Did you ask her about the internship programme?

script.speaker: B
script.text: Yes, and she said I should apply by the end of this month.

questions.stem: What did the woman learn about the internship programme?
options: It was cancelled for this year.
She needs more credits to apply.
She should apply by the end of the month.
The advisor will nominate her directly.
questions.explanation: The woman says the advisor told her to apply by the end of this month.

script.speaker: A
script.text: I'm thinking of renting a bike to get around the city.

script.speaker: B
script.text: That's a good idea. There's a rental shop near the station.

script.speaker: A
script.text: Do you know how much it costs per day?

script.speaker: B
script.text: Around fifteen dollars, and they include a helmet and lock.

questions.stem: What does the man say about the bike rental?
options: It is cheaper for weekly rentals.
It includes a helmet and lock.
It requires a deposit.
It is located near the park.
questions.explanation: The man says the rental includes a helmet and lock.

script.speaker: A
script.text: How are things going with your new roommates?

script.speaker: B
script.text: Mostly good, but one of them never does the dishes.

script.speaker: A
script.text: Have you talked to them about it?

script.speaker: B
script.text: I left a note on the fridge. If it doesn't improve, I'll bring it up directly.

questions.stem: What has the woman done about the dishes problem so far?
options: She spoke to the roommate directly.
She asked the landlord to intervene.
She left a note on the fridge.
She decided to let it go.
questions.explanation: The woman says she left a note on the fridge about the dishes.

script.speaker: A
script.text: I heard you started volunteering at the museum. How is it?

script.speaker: B
script.text: It's fascinating. I'm helping them catalogue old photographs.

script.speaker: A
script.text: That sounds like detailed work. How many hours a week do you do?

script.speaker: B
script.text: Only six, but I wish I had more time to spend there.

questions.stem: What does the woman do as a museum volunteer?
options: She gives guided tours to visitors.
She helps catalogue old photographs.
She organises special exhibitions.
She works at the ticket counter.
questions.explanation: The woman says she is helping them catalogue old photographs.

script.speaker: A
script.text: The water in my apartment has been brown all morning.

script.speaker: B
script.text: That sounds like a pipe issue. Did you call the building manager?

script.speaker: A
script.text: I did, but he said several units are having the same problem.

script.speaker: B
script.text: There must be maintenance work on the mainline. It should clear by evening.

questions.stem: What does the man suspect is causing the water problem?
options: A leak in the woman's apartment.
Maintenance work on the main water line.
The building manager turned off the water.
A storm damaged the pipes.
questions.explanation: The man suspects maintenance work on the mainline is causing the issue.

script.speaker: A
script.text: I'm taking over the night shift. How is the patient in room 204?

script.speaker: B
script.text: Stable, but he had a fever spike around two this afternoon.

script.speaker: A
script.text: Did the doctor adjust his medication?

script.speaker: B
script.text: Yes, and his temperature has been normal since about four o'clock.

questions.stem: What change occurred with the patient's condition?
options: He was moved to a different room.
He had a fever spike in the afternoon.
He was discharged from the hospital.
He refused to take his medication.
questions.explanation: The nurse reports the patient had a fever spike around two in the afternoon.

script.speaker: A
script.text: What are you listening to while you walk?

script.speaker: B
script.text: A podcast about urban farming. It's really interesting.

script.speaker: A
script.text: I've been meaning to get into podcasts, but I never know where to start.

script.speaker: B
script.text: Try this one — I can send you a link. It's only twenty minutes per episode.

questions.stem: What does the man offer to do?
options: Walk with the woman tomorrow.
Recommend a different podcast.
Send the woman a link to the podcast.
Lend the woman his headphones.
questions.explanation: The man offers to send the woman a link to the podcast.

script.speaker: A
script.text: Have you heard about the office relocation?

script.speaker: B
script.text: Yes, we're moving to the third floor next month.

script.speaker: A
script.text: I hope the new space has better lighting. It's so dark in here.

script.speaker: B
script.text: Apparently the new office has large windows and a view of the river.

questions.stem: What does the woman complain about in the current office?
options: It is too noisy.
It has poor lighting.
It is too small.
It is far from public transport.
questions.explanation: The woman says the lighting in the current office is dark.

script.speaker: A
script.text: Are you going to the guest lecture on climate policy tonight?

script.speaker: B
script.text: I really want to, but I have a group project meeting at the same time.

script.speaker: A
script.text: That's too bad. The speaker is a well-known environmental scientist.

script.speaker: B
script.text: I know. I'll try to watch the recording if they post it online.

questions.stem: Why can't the man attend the guest lecture?
options: He has to study for an exam.
He has a group project meeting.
He is not interested in the topic.
He has to work late.
questions.explanation: The man says he has a group project meeting at the same time as the lecture.

script.speaker: A
script.text: I tried to visit the museum today, but it was closed.

script.speaker: B
script.text: They're closed on Mondays for maintenance. It says so on their website.

script.speaker: A
script.text: I should have checked before walking all the way there.

script.speaker: B
script.text: At least it's free entry on the first Sunday of every month.

questions.stem: Why was the museum closed?
options: It is being renovated.
It is closed on Mondays for maintenance.
There is a staff shortage today.
It is closed for a private event.
questions.explanation: The man says the museum is closed on Mondays for maintenance.

script.speaker: A
script.text: We need to review the safety procedures before the lab opens.

script.speaker: B
script.text: I already read the manual, but a quick recap would help.

script.speaker: A
script.text: The main thing is to wear goggles at all times and know where the fire exit is.

script.speaker: B
script.text: And we should never leave chemicals unattended. I remember that one.

questions.stem: What is the woman's main safety instruction?
options: Keep the lab door locked.
Wear goggles and know the fire exit location.
Read the manual before each session.
Store chemicals in labelled containers.
questions.explanation: The woman says the main thing is to wear goggles at all times and know where the fire exit is.

script.speaker: A
script.text: Did you know that some birds sleep while they're flying?

script.speaker: B
script.text: Really? How is that possible without crashing?

script.speaker: A
script.text: They shut down one half of their brain at a time. It's called unihemispheric sleep.

script.speaker: B
script.text: That's amazing. I wish I could do that during long study sessions.

questions.stem: What does the woman explain about bird sleep?
options: Birds sleep more during winter.
Birds sleep with one half of their brain at a time.
Birds only sleep on the ground.
Birds need less sleep than mammals.
questions.explanation: The woman explains that birds shut down one half of their brain at a time.

script.speaker: A
script.text: I've been getting sick a lot lately. I wonder why.

script.speaker: B
script.text: You've been under a lot of stress with your thesis deadline.

script.speaker: A
script.text: Does stress really weaken your immune system that much?

script.speaker: B
script.text: Absolutely. Chronic stress raises cortisol levels, which lowers your body's defences.

questions.stem: What does the man say about the link between stress and illness?
options: Stress only affects mental health, not physical health.
Chronic stress raises cortisol levels, weakening defences.
Exercise can completely prevent stress-related illness.
Getting sick is unrelated to stress levels.
questions.explanation: The man explains that chronic stress raises cortisol levels, which lowers the body's defences.

script.speaker: A
script.text: We're studying the printing press in my history class. It's fascinating.

script.speaker: B
script.text: Invented by Gutenberg, right? It revolutionised access to books.

script.speaker: A
script.text: Exactly. Before that, books were copied by hand and only the wealthy could afford them.

script.speaker: B
script.text: It's hard to imagine a world without printed books now.

questions.stem: According to the conversation, why was the printing press so important?
options: It made books cheaper and more accessible.
It was the first machine to use ink.
It helped authors write faster.
It eliminated the need for libraries.
questions.explanation: The woman says before the printing press, books were copied by hand and only the wealthy could afford them, implying it made books more accessible.

script.speaker: A
script.text: I signed up for a cooking class next weekend.

script.speaker: B
script.text: That sounds fun. What kind of cuisine?

script.speaker: A
script.text: Italian. We're learning to make pasta from scratch and a classic tomato sauce.

script.speaker: B
script.text: Can I come with you? I've always wanted to learn homemade pasta.

questions.stem: What will the cooking class focus on?
options: French pastry techniques.
Italian pasta and tomato sauce.
Asian stir-fry dishes.
Basic knife skills.
questions.explanation: The woman says they are learning to make pasta from scratch and a classic tomato sauce.

script.speaker: A
script.text: My online order still hasn't arrived, and it's been two weeks.

script.speaker: B
script.text: Did you get a tracking number when you placed the order?

script.speaker: A
script.text: No, I chose the standard shipping option. I'm starting to regret it.

script.speaker: B
script.text: You should contact customer support. They might offer a refund or replacement.

questions.stem: What does the man advise the woman to do?
options: Cancel the order and buy from another store.
Contact customer support about the situation.
Wait another week for the delivery.
Choose express shipping next time.
questions.explanation: The man advises the woman to contact customer support for a refund or replacement.

script.speaker: A
script.text: Have you seen the new community garden on Fifth Street?

script.speaker: B
script.text: Yes, it's lovely. They turned an empty lot into vegetable beds.

script.speaker: A
script.text: I heard local residents can rent a plot for a small fee.

script.speaker: B
script.text: Exactly. It's a great way to grow fresh food and meet neighbours.

questions.stem: What is special about the new community garden?
options: It is run by a professional landscaping company.
It was built on an empty lot and residents can rent plots.
It only grows flowers, not vegetables.
It is free for anyone to use without registration.
questions.explanation: The garden was created on an empty lot, and residents can rent a plot for a small fee.
